rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Further Motion For Attorneys Fees As To Defendants Christie West And Timothy West
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2026. Line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Further Motion For Attorneys Fees as to Defendants Christie West and Timothy West is DENIED without prejudice for Plaintiff to re-file segregating the fees as much as possible and providing detailed explanation as to the fees that cannot be segregated as to each category listed in the moving Memorandum of Points and Authorities 4:27-6:1. The Court notes that arguments provided in the moving papers for the reasons supporting joint fee award are limited and arguments advanced for the first time in reply are not persuasive and do not address each category anyway. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Termination And/Or Stay Of Pending Sheriff Sales And Removal Of Abstract Of Judgment And Writs From The 1215 29th Avenue Property As To Christie West
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2026. Line 1. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY WEST Motion For Termination And/Or Stay Of Pending Sheriff Sales And Removal Of Abstract Of Judgment And Writs From The 1215 29th Avenue Property As To Christie West is OFF CALENDAR. No notice of motion on file. It also is not clear based on the filings with this Court if the version of the Motion filed on April 21, 2026 has been served on the Plaintiff. Proof of Service accompanying April 21, 2026 filing appears to be identical to the proof of service attached to the attempted April 17, 2026 filing without any additional information or explanation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LISA RAMSEY-SIOTTO VS. TEN MILLER PLACE CONDOMINIUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2026. Line 6. PLAINTIFF LISA RAMSEY-SIOTTO INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE LISA RAMSEY SURVIVORS TRUST CREATED UTD 6/16/2016, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is continued to June 30, 2026. No later than June 12, 2026 moving party to file a supplemental brief and/or declaration addressing the propriety of issuance of a preliminary injunction on a motion against (1) defendants (Precision Security Solutions, d/b/a National Protective Services) that have not appeared in the action or provide evidence that these defendants appeared in this action; (2) defendants that have not been served with the summons and complaint or provide evidence of service; (3) defendants that have not been served with the motion for preliminary injunction or provide evidence of service. See Notice of Motion filed on March 3, 2026 seeking injunction against Precision Security Solutions, d/b/a National Protective Services (2:5-6); Proof of Service for the Motion for Preliminary Injunction filed on March 3, 2026; Register of Actions showing no proof of service of Summons and Complaint on Precision Security Solutions, d/b/a National Protective Services or any filings by Precision Security Solutions, d/b/a National Protective Services. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
